name: Jean Goldkette
abstract: John Jean Goldkette (March 18, 1893 – March 24, 1962) was a jazz pianist and bandleader.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates that Jean Goldkette hired Don Redmon and the McKinney Cottonpickers, but the interviewee (Butter Jackson) is not described as having a direct personal or professional relationship with Goldkette.

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Chauncey Morehouse) does not mention any personal interaction, collaboration, or direct relationship with Jean Goldkette. The interviewer narrates the history of Goldkette's association with the interviewee's father and brother, but the interviewee only confirms his father's name an

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: I won't say better, but way ahead, advanced. 'Cause I swear, I'm always thinkin' advanced. I always listened to things, the things that knocked me out was the ones that was more advanced all the time.

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: I wound up, some fellow had a band in 1936. Normally you would know about Hank Biagini — well Hank Biagini was from Detroit and he had the original Casa Loma band, which was actually Jean Goldkette
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I would walk to, to the loop with Chicago, and I see the, the different theaters, the famous bands as come through. Jean Goldkette, Wayne King, Ted Lewis and many, many famous big names.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: the first time we heard a string bass player was Steve, a white boy who came to New York with Jean Goldkette's band... go up and hear Goldkette's band.
